message:
perf(core): drop too-strict memdb_rows_loaded guard from OPT-B2 fast path

Follow-up to 762d125d. The original gate included `!memdb_rows_loaded`,
which was meant to preserve "don't skip when the mirror has never been
loaded" safety. The reload function's own tail (line 43051) sets
`memdb_rows_loaded.set(true)` unconditionally on every successful full
reload, so after the first reload on a long-lived connection the flag is
always true and the fast path never fired.

For the hydrate_rows=false case, the full reload path gates user-table row
scanning on `hydrate_rows || name.eq_ignore_ascii_case("sqlite_sequence")`
(see should_scan_rows lower in this function), so the memdb's row image is
identical across reloads no matter what memdb_rows_loaded reports. Dropping
this guard is therefore safe: schema_cookie equality already guarantees the
schema image is identical, and the remaining guards still fence off pending
direct upserts, forced reloads, and vtab preservation.

File-backed strict-no-fallback workloads (the primary user of this path in
release builds) now see the steady-state fast-path return on every commit's
post-boundary refresh.

Self-connection ryow tests pass. perf_update_delete :memory: workload does
not exercise this path (hydrate_rows=true on :memory:), so its numbers are
unchanged as expected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
